perturbation.original_text: The Adviser hereby agrees to indemnify and save harmless FASC and its trustees, officers and employees (each of the foregoing an "Indemnified Party" and collectively the "Indemnified Parties") against and from any and all losses, obligations, penalties, actions, judgments and suits and other costs, expenses and disbursements of any kind or nature whatsoever which may be imposed on, incurred by or asserted against an Indemnified Party, other than as a consequence of gross negligence or willful misconduct on the part of an Indemnified Party, arising out of or in connection with this Limited Power of Attorney or any other agreement, instrument or document executed in connection with the exercise of the authority granted to FASC herein to act on behalf of the Adviser, including without limitation the reasonable costs, expenses and disbursements in connection with defending such Indemnified Party against any claim or liability related to the exercise or performance of any of FASC's powers or duties under this Limited Power of Attorney or any of the other agreements, instruments or documents executed in connection with the exercise of the authority granted to FASC herein to act on behalf of the Adviser, or the taking of any action under or in connection with any of the foregoing. The obligations of the Adviser under this paragraph shall survive the termination of this Limited Power of Attorney with respect to actions taken by FASC on behalf of the Adviser during the term of this Limited Power of Attorney.
perturbation.changed_text: The Adviser hereby agrees to indemnify and save harmless FASC and its trustees, officers and employees (each of the foregoing an "Indemnified Party" and collectively the "Indemnified Parties") against and from any and all losses, obligations, penalties, actions, judgments and suits and other costs, expenses and disbursements of any kind or nature whatsoever which may be imposed on, incurred by or asserted against an Indemnified Party, other than as a consequence of gross negligence or willful misconduct on the part of an Indemnified Party, arising out of or in connection with this Limited Power of Attorney or any other agreement, instrument or document executed in connection with the exercise of the authority granted to FASC herein to act on behalf of the Adviser, including without limitation the reasonable costs, expenses and disbursements in connection with defending such Indemnified Party against any claim or liability related to the exercise or performance of any of FASC's powers or duties under this Limited Power of Attorney or any of the other agreements, instruments or documents executed in connection with the exercise of the authority granted to FASC herein to act on behalf of the Adviser, or the taking of any action under or in connection with any of the foregoing. The obligations of the Adviser under this paragraph shall not survive the termination of this Limited Power of Attorney with respect to actions taken by FASC on behalf of the Adviser during the term of this Limited Power of Attorney, unless explicitly stated otherwise in a separate written agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The changed text creates an in-text contradiction by stating that the indemnification obligations of the Adviser do not survive the termination of the Limited Power of Attorney, unless otherwise stated in another agreement. This contradicts the original text, which states that these obligations *shall* survive termination. This introduces uncertainty regarding the extent of the Adviser's liability after termination, especially concerning actions taken by FASC during the term of the agreement. This creates ambiguity in the scope of Indemnification to be applied and when to apply it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Adviser hereby agrees to indemnify and save harmless FASC and its trustees, officers and employees (each of the foregoing an "Indemnified Party" and collectively the "Indemnified Parties") against and from any and all losses, obligations, penalties, actions, judgments and suits and other costs, expenses and disbursements of any kind or nature whatsoever which may be imposed on, incurred by or asserted against an Indemnified Party, other than as a consequence of gross negligence or willful misconduct on the part of an Indemnified Party, arising out of or in connection with this Limited Power of Attorney or any other agreement, instrument or document executed in connection with the exercise of the authority granted to FASC herein to act on behalf of the Adviser, including without limitation the reasonable costs, expenses and disbursements in connection with defending such Indemnified Party against any claim or liability related to the exercise or performance of any of FASC's powers or duties under this Limited Power of Attorney or any of the other agreements, instruments or documents executed in connection with the exercise of the authority granted to FASC herein to act on behalf of the Adviser, or the taking of any action under or in connection with any of the foregoing. The obligations of the Adviser under this paragraph shall not survive the termination of this Limited Power of Attorney with respect to actions taken by FASC on behalf of the Adviser during the term of this Limited Power of Attorney, unless explicitly stated otherwise in a separate written agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original statement regarding the survival of indemnification obligations. The original text states that the obligations *shall* survive termination, while the changed text states that the obligations *shall not* survive termination unless otherwise agreed. This creates a clear and direct conflict regarding the extent of the Adviser's liability after termination, leading to ambiguity in the scope of indemnification. The Contradicted text is identical to the changed text, thus also contradicts the original text provided.

perturbation.original_text: Any person, partnership, corporation or other legal entity dealing with FASC in its capacity as attorney-in-fact hereunder for the Adviser on behalf of any Fund is hereby expressly put on notice that FASC is acting solely in the capacity as an agent of the Adviser as agent for the Fund and that any such person, partnership, corporation or other legal entity must look solely to the Fund in question for enforcement of any claim against the Fund, as FASC assumes no personal liability whatsoever for obligations of the Fund entered into by FASC in its capacity as attorney-in-fact for the Adviser.
perturbation.changed_text: Any person, partnership, corporation or other legal entity dealing with FASC in its capacity as attorney-in-fact hereunder for the Adviser on behalf of any Fund is hereby expressly put on notice that FASC is acting solely in the capacity as an agent of the Adviser as agent for the Fund, and that any such person, partnership, corporation or other legal entity may, at their discretion, seek recourse from either the Fund in question or directly from FASC for enforcement of any claim related to actions undertaken by FASC on behalf of the Fund. FASC's liability will be determined on a case-by-case basis, considering the specific circumstances of the transaction.
perturbation.explanation: The changed text creates an in-text contradiction regarding FASC's liability. The original text explicitly states that FASC assumes no personal liability and that parties must look solely to the Fund for enforcement of claims. The changed text introduces the possibility of seeking recourse directly from FASC and implies that FASC may be liable, depending on the circumstances. This undermines the original protection afforded to FASC and creates ambiguity regarding liability for obligations entered into on behalf of the Fund.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Any person, partnership, corporation or other legal entity dealing with FASC in its capacity as attorney-in-fact hereunder for the Adviser on behalf of any Fund is hereby expressly put on notice that FASC is acting solely in the capacity as an agent of the Adviser as agent for the Fund, and that any such person, partnership, corporation or other legal entity may, at their discretion, seek recourse from either the Fund in question or directly from FASC for enforcement of any claim related to actions undertaken by FASC on behalf of the Fund. FASC's liability will be determined on a case-by-case basis, considering the specific circumstances of the transaction. Each person, partnership, corporation or other legal entity which deals with a Fund through FASC in its capacity as agent and attorney-in-fact of the Adviser, is hereby expressly put on notice (i) that all persons or entities dealing with the Fund must look solely to the assets of the Fund on whose behalf FASC is acting pursuant to its powers hereunder for enforcement of any claim against the Fund, as the trustees, officers and/or agents of such Fund, the shareholders of the various classes of shares of the Fund, and the other Funds of the trust or corporation of which a Fund may be a series, assume no personal liability whatsoever for obligations entered into on behalf of such Fund, and (ii) that the rights, liabilities and obligations of any one Fund are separate and distinct from those of any other Fund.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text states that FASC assumes no personal liability, while the changed text states that parties 'may, at their discretion, seek recourse from either the Fund in question or directly from FASC' and that 'FASC's liability will be determined on a case-by-case basis.' This contradicts the earlier statement of no liability. The contradicted text, which is now a duplicate of the changed text, itself contains a contradiction by stating both that FASC may be liable and that all persons dealing with the Fund must look solely to the assets of the Fund for enforcement of any claim against the Fund, as the trustees, officers and/or agents of such Fund assume no personal liability whatsoever for obligations entered into on behalf of such Fund. This inconsistency creates ambiguity and uncertainty, fitting the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: This Limited Power of Attorney shall be revoked and terminated automatically upon the cancellation or termination of the Services Agreement or as to any Fund upon the cancellation or termination of the Adviser's Investment Advisory Contract for such Fund. Except as provided in the immediately preceding sentence, the powers and authorities herein granted may be revoked or terminated by the Adviser at any time provided that no such revocation or termination shall be effective until FASC has received actual notice of such revocation or termination in writing from the Adviser.
perturbation.changed_text: This Limited Power of Attorney shall be revoked and terminated automatically upon the cancellation or termination of the Services Agreement or as to any Fund upon the cancellation or termination of the Adviser's Investment Advisory Contract for such Fund. The powers and authorities herein granted may be revoked or terminated by the Adviser at any time with or without prior notice to FASC.
perturbation.explanation: The changed text introduces an in-text contradiction by removing the requirement for *written* notice for revocation. The original text states that revocation is only effective upon FASC receiving *written* notice. The revised text allows for revocation 'at any time with or without prior notice,' effectively negating the requirement for written notice and introducing uncertainty about the revocation process. This creates ambiguity regarding the valid method for terminating the power of attorney and could lead to disputes over unauthorized actions taken by FASC after an uncommunicated revocation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Limited Power of Attorney shall be revoked and terminated automatically upon the cancellation or termination of the Services Agreement or as to any Fund upon the cancellation or termination of the Adviser's Investment Advisory Contract for such Fund. The powers and authorities herein granted may be revoked or terminated by the Adviser at any time with or without prior notice to FASC. This Limited Power of Attorney constitutes the entire agreement between the Adviser and FASC and may be changed only by a writing signed by both of them, except that the Adviser may at any time change the list of Funds to which this Limited Power of Attorney relates by executing and delivering to FASC a later dated version of Schedule 1.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the requirement for written notice for revocation of the Limited Power of Attorney. The original text states that revocation is only effective upon FASC receiving written notice. The revised text allows for revocation 'at any time with or without prior notice to FASC,' effectively negating the requirement for written notice. Since the contradicted text repeats the changed text, it also contains the contradiction of stating both that prior notice may or may not be given.
